children.name: about_me.txt
children.content:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         PRAVIN PRAJAPATI                     ║
║     AI / ML Engineer & Security Researcher   ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Location: Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India
Focus: Machine Learning, Document AI, Cyber Security

Current Research:
  • Invoice extraction using CNN + NLP pipelines
  • Phishing URL detection with gradient boosting
  • OCR preprocessing with adaptive thresholding
  • Malware hash classification

Stack:
  Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn, OpenCV
  Django, FastAPI, React, TypeScript
  Docker, AWS, PostgreSQL, Redis

Publications:
  • "Adaptive Noise Removal for Document OCR" — 2024
  • "URL-Based Phishing Detection with Feature Engineering" — 2023

Contact: pravin@researcher.dev
GitHub: github.com/pravin-python
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/pravin

children.name: model_logs.txt
children.content:
═══════════════════════════════════════════
  MODEL TRAINING LOG — InvoiceExtractor-v3
═══════════════════════════════════════════

Model: InvoiceExtractor-v3
Framework: PyTorch 2.1
Architecture: ResNet-50 + LSTM decoder
Dataset Size: 18,000 annotated invoice images
Train/Val/Test Split: 70/15/15

Training Configuration:
  Optimizer: AdamW (lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)
  Scheduler: CosineAnnealingLR
  Batch Size: 32
  Epochs: 50
  Augmentation: rotation, perspective warp, noise

Final Metrics:
  Accuracy:  94.2%
  Precision: 92.7%
  Recall:    95.1%
  F1-Score:  93.9%

Epoch History (last 10):
  Epoch 41 | loss: 0.0821 | val_acc: 93.1%

children.name: model_config.yaml
children.content:
# InvoiceExtractor-v3 Configuration

model:
  name: InvoiceExtractor-v3
  architecture: resnet50_lstm
  pretrained: true
  num_classes: 12
  hidden_size: 512
  dropout: 0.3

training:
  epochs: 50
  batch_size: 32
  learning_rate: 0.0003
  weight_decay: 0.01
  optimizer: adamw
  scheduler: cosine_annealing
  warmup_epochs: 5

data:
  train_dir: /datasets/invoices/train
  val_dir: /datasets/invoices/val
  test_dir: /datasets/invoices/test
  image_size: [640, 480]
  augmentation:
    - random_rotation: 15
    - perspective_warp: 0.1
    - gaussian_noise: 0.02
    - color_jitter: 0.2

inference:
  confidence_threshold: 0.85
  nms_threshold: 0.5
  max_detections: 50

children.name: ocr_experiment.txt
children.content:
╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗
║  EXPERIMENT: OCR Pipeline v2.1       ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝

Objective:
  Build a robust OCR pipeline for extracting text
  from scanned invoices with varying quality.

Preprocessing Steps:
  1. Grayscale conversion
  2. Adaptive thresholding (Gaussian, blockSize=11)
  3. Morphological operations (dilate + erode)
  4. Deskew using Hough transform
  5. Noise removal with bilateral filtering

Text Detection:
  • EAST text detector for region proposals
  • Non-maximum suppression (threshold=0.5)
  • Bounding box refinement with padding

Recognition:
  • Tesseract OCR with LSTM engine
  • Custom trained model for tabular data
  • Post-processing: regex for amounts, dates

Results:
  Character accuracy: 96.8%
  Word accuracy: 92.3%
  Field extraction: 89.1%

Conclusion:
  Adaptive thresholding + deskew improved
  accuracy by 12% on low-quality scans.
  Next: fine-tune on domain-specific fonts.

children.name: barcode_detection.txt
children.content:
╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗
║  EXPERIMENT: Barcode Detection       ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝

Objective:
  Detect and decode 1D/2D barcodes from
  document images in real-time.

Approach:
  1. Region proposal with gradient analysis
  2. Scharr gradient (horizontal + vertical)
  3. Morphological closing to merge regions
  4. Contour detection + aspect ratio filter
  5. ZBar library for decoding

Supported Formats:
  • EAN-13, EAN-8
  • Code-128, Code-39
  • QR Code
  • Data Matrix

Performance:
  Detection rate: 97.2%
  Decode success: 94.8%
  Avg latency: 45ms per image
  False positive rate: 1.3%

Edge Cases Handled:
  • Rotated barcodes (up to 45°)
  • Partially occluded codes
  • Multiple codes in single image
  • Low contrast scenarios

children.name: document_parser.txt
children.content:
╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗
║  EXPERIMENT: Document Parser v3      ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝

Objective:
  Extract structured data from unstructured
  business documents (invoices, receipts, POs).

Architecture:
  Document → Layout Analysis → OCR → NER → Structured Output

Layout Analysis:
  • LayoutLM v3 for document understanding
  • Segment detection: header, table, footer
  • Reading order determination

Named Entity Recognition:
  Entities: vendor_name, invoice_number, date,
            line_items, subtotal, tax, total,
            payment_terms, due_date

Training Data:
  • 18,000 invoice images (annotated)
  • 5,200 receipts
  • 3,100 purchase orders

Results by Document Type:
  Invoices:  F1 = 93.9%
  Receipts:  F1 = 91.2%
  POs:       F1 = 89.7%

Deployment:
  • FastAPI serving endpoint
  • Batch processing support
  • 200ms avg inference time
  • Horizontal scaling via K8s
